title: Collaborative repository capstone deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Collaborative repository capstone; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Collaborative repository capstone: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Installing Git and granting repository access is enough to make collaboration predictable and safe.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Make responsibilities, lifecycle, evidence, permissions, communication, and exceptions explicit, then test the contributor path and update it when practice changes.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Make responsibilities, lifecycle, evidence, permissions, communication, and exceptions explicit, then test the contributor path and update it when practice changes.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Collaborative repository capstone instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Make responsibilities, lifecycle, evidence, permissions, communication, and exceptions explicit, then test the contributor path and update it when practice changes.
Installing Git and granting repository access is enough to make collaboration predictable and safe.

answer.explanation: Make responsibilities, lifecycle, evidence, permissions, communication, and exceptions explicit, then test the contributor path and update it when practice changes.
